creator: sean@rogue-research.com
text:
So then this is not fixed.

We should be careful to not conflate:

1. merely *building* the port (on a dedicated build machine)
2. what the resulting build can itself do at runtime when installed elsewhere

From https://v8.dev/blog/jitless we see there can be a big performance hit at runtime with a jitless build. So while it's great that the FreeBSD port now has this this jitless option, it's not what this ticket is about.

I don't mind having the JIT on my production web server.  I just want my build machine to be able to be more locked down, i.e. with w^x mode enabled.

You can cross compile FreeBSD *for* ARM *on* Intel, so what I'm seeking should be possible too.
